methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series E)

data.narrative:
Divergent operates in Robotics & Manufacturing at Series E with $600M+ raised and a last post-money valuation of $2.3B. The comparable universe comprises eight peers: one public company (277810.KQ) and seven private robotics firms, including Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Locus Robotics ($2.0B, Series F), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a range. Stage-progression applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement to the $2.3B base, implying $3.2B. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables yields $1.7B, with an observed range of $1.0B–$2.0B, reflecting stage and maturity dispersion within the cohort. The stage-progression method anchors to the company's demonstrated fundraising trajectory; the peer-median method grounds valuation in external market reference points.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated range of $1.4B (low) to $3.7B (high), with a median of $2.5B. This narrow band around the current $2.3B valuation reflects consistency between internal funding progression and peer comparables. Key uncertainties include stage timing to Series F or later (which could justify the higher range), and variability in robotics peer valuations driven by disparate business models and near-term capital efficiency.
